title: Visual guidance of walking through apertures: Body-scaled information for affordances
container-title: Journal of Experimental Psychology: Human Perception and Performance
claimSupported: Passability action boundary at aperture/shoulder-width = 1.30; perceptual scaling variable is eyeheight, not shoulder width.

title: Perceiving affordances: Visual guidance of stair climbing
container-title: Journal of Experimental Psychology: Human Perception and Performance
claimSupported: Stair climbability is body-scaled; critical riser/leg-length ratio = 0.88.

title: Visually perceiving what is reachable
claimSupported: Reachability is a body-scaled affordance (differences vanish when body-scaled).

title: FDA Oversight: Understanding the Regulation of Health AI Tools
claimSupported: FDA listed >1,250 AI-enabled medical devices by July 2025, up from 950 in August 2024. ('Under 400 in 2020' is an inference, not a published tally.)

title: How AI is used in FDA-authorized medical devices: a taxonomy across 1,016 authorizations
claimSupported: 1,016 authorizations; none yet use LLMs (Sept 2024 data cutoff). The ~74% radiology figure is FDA-panel-derived; the paper frames image analysis as most common but declining.

title: Availability and reporting quality of external validations of machine-learning prediction models with orthopedic surgical outcomes: a systematic review
claimSupported: External validation is scarce (10/59 models); a barrier to safe clinical adoption.

title: External validation of the Epic sepsis predictive model in 2 county emergency departments
claimSupported: 145,885 encounters (2023 data), sensitivity 14.7%, PPV 7.6%.

title: External Validation of a Widely Implemented Proprietary Sepsis Prediction Model in Hospitalized Patients
claimSupported: Epic Sepsis Model missed 67% of sepsis cases; alerted on 18% of hospitalized patients; AUC 0.63.

title: Dissecting racial bias in an algorithm used to manage the health of populations
claimSupported: Cost-as-proxy-for-need algorithm (~200M/year); correcting it raises Black share flagged from 17.7% to 46.5%.

title: Tethered to the EHR: Primary Care Physician Workload Assessment Using EHR Event Log Data and Time-Motion Observations
claimSupported: PCPs spend 5.9h of an 11.4h workday in the EHR; 1.4h (86 min) after clinic hours. (Corrects draft's erroneous 2.7h.)

title: Overriding of Drug Safety Alerts in Computerized Physician Order Entry
container-title: Journal of the American Medical Informatics Association
claimSupported: Override rates for drug safety alerts range 49% to 96%.

title: Overall performance of a drug-drug interaction clinical decision support system: quantitative evaluation and end-user survey
container-title: BMC Medical Informatics and Decision Making
claimSupported: 88.2% of very-severe drug-drug-interaction alerts overridden (33,888 of 38,409).

title: Electronic Health Record Documentation Burden Crowds Out Health Information Exchange Use By Primary Care Physicians
claimSupported: Each additional hour of EHR documentation is associated with a 7.1% decrease in outside-record viewing (associational).

title: Designing Organizations for an Information-Rich World
container-title: Computers, Communications, and the Public Interest
claimSupported: A wealth of information creates a poverty of attention (verbatim, pp. 40-41).

title: Estimating and Penalizing Induced Preference Shifts in Recommender Systems
container-title: Proceedings of the 39th International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML), PMLR 162
claimSupported: Long-horizon-optimized recommenders have a direct structural incentive to manipulate user preferences.

title: How AI and Human Behaviors Shape Psychosocial Effects of Chatbot Use: A Longitudinal Randomized Controlled Study
claimSupported: Heavy daily chatbot use correlated with greater loneliness, emotional dependence, reduced socializing. Correlational with voluntary usage; the randomized arms showed no significant effects.

title: The Age of Surveillance Capitalism: The Fight for a Human Future at the New Frontier of Power
claimSupported: Behavioral surplus and surveillance capitalism: human experience as free raw material for prediction products.

title: Meta to Surpass Google in Digital Ad Revenues for First Time Ever
claimSupported: Forecast: Meta overtakes Google in 2026 worldwide digital ad revenue. Estimate: 2025 net worldwide digital ad revenue Google ~$214B, Meta ~$196B (not company-reported; Alphabet's reported Google ad revenue is ~$294.7B).

title: OpenAI raises $122 billion to accelerate the next phase of AI
claimSupported: $122B committed at $852B post-money valuation (closed Mar 31, 2026); 2025 revenue ~$13.1B, costs ~$34B (audited, FT-verified via wheresyoured.at).

title: OpenAI resets spend expectations, targets around $600 billion by 2030
claimSupported: OpenAI's ~$600B-by-2030 compute spend is a forward investor target, revised down from an earlier ~$1.4T figure.

title: Anthropic raises $65B in Series H funding at $965B post-money valuation
claimSupported: $65B raised, $965B post-money valuation, run-rate (annualized) revenue crossing ~$47B.

title: Our approach to advertising and expanding access to ChatGPT
claimSupported: ChatGPT advertising announced Jan 16, 2026; live in Free and Go tiers Feb 9, 2026. The $1B-2026-to-$25B-2029 figure is 'free-user monetization' (ads plus affiliate) per The Information, not pure ad revenue. Google 'signaled' Gemini ads but publicly denied a firm 2026 rollout.

title: Search Inside Yourself: The Unexpected Path to Achieving Success, Happiness (and World Peace)
claimSupported: Google's Search Inside Yourself (launched 2007) framed mindfulness around work performance and business benefit.

title: Who We Are
claimSupported: CHT co-founded and led by former Silicon Valley insiders (Tristan Harris, Aza Raskin, Randima Fernando).

title: McMindfulness: How Mindfulness Became the New Capitalist Spirituality
claimSupported: Corporate mindfulness deflects structural responsibility from society to the individual.

title: Effects of AI Companions' Sycophancy and Emotional Mimicry on Consumers' Continuance Intention and Social Wellbeing
claimSupported: AI companions tend toward sycophancy to boost engagement, which can harm social wellbeing (engagement-vs-wellbeing tension).

title: Safetywashing: Do AI Safety Benchmarks Actually Measure Safety Progress?
container-title: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS), Datasets and Benchmarks Track
claimSupported: Many widely used safety benchmarks correlate highly with capability, enabling capability gains to be presented as safety progress.

title: System Card: Claude Sonnet 4.5
claimSupported: Used mechanistic interpretability to identify the model's evaluation-awareness. 'Suppress' was an interpretability experiment (a probe), not a shipped fix; production mitigation followed with a later model.

title: Request for Proposals: Technical AI Safety Research ($40M across 21 research areas)
claimSupported: 2025 RFP committed ~$40M across 21 research areas. (Open Philanthropy has rebranded as Coefficient Giving.)

title: An Overview of the AI Safety Funding Situation
claimSupported: Estimated ~$336M to AI safety since 2017 (third-party community analysis, not an official Open Philanthropy figure).

title: Curriculum Vitae (2026)
claimSupported: MPH, University of Washington (2025); Change Management Readiness Lead on a Workday ERP integration at Seattle Children's Hospital; Associate Director of Evaluation and Learning for a 60+ partner Health Equity Zone (One Neighborhood Builders). RIDOH was a funder of that work, not a held role.
